flashcards.front: What is an Atomic Commit Protocol?
flashcards.back: A protocol ensuring that in a distributed transaction spanning multiple nodes, either ALL nodes commit the transaction or NONE do. It guarantees atomicity in distributed environments.

flashcards.front: What is the Two-Phase Commit (2PC) protocol?
flashcards.back: A atomic commit protocol with two phases: Vote (participants vote commit/abort) and Decision (coordinator makes global decision). Ensures all-or-nothing execution across distributed databases.

flashcards.front: What is the Three-Phase Commit (3PC) protocol?
flashcards.back: A non-blocking atomic commit protocol with three phases: CanCommit, PreCommit, and DoCommit. Designed to avoid blocking when coordinator fails.

flashcards.front: What is the role of Coordinator in 2PC?
flashcards.back: The coordinator initiates the transaction, sends commands to participants, collects votes, makes global commit/abort decision, and informs all participants of the final outcome.

flashcards.front: What is the role of Participant in 2PC?
flashcards.back: Participants execute the transaction, vote commit or abort in vote phase, and execute coordinator's final decision (commit or abort) in decision phase.

flashcards.front: What happens in the Vote Phase of 2PC?
flashcards.back: Coordinator sends VOTE-REQUEST to all participants. Each participant executes transaction, writes undo/redo logs, and responds with VOTE-COMMIT or VOTE-ABORT.

flashcards.front: What happens in the Decision Phase of 2PC?
flashcards.back: Coordinator collects votes. If all vote commit, sends GLOBAL-COMMIT; if any votes abort, sends GLOBAL-ABORT. Participants then commit or abort accordingly.

flashcards.front: What is the blocking problem in 2PC?
flashcards.back: If coordinator fails after participants vote commit, participants block (wait indefinitely) because they don't know the global decision. They cannot release locks or proceed.

flashcards.front: How does 3PC solve the blocking problem?
flashcards.back: 3PC adds a Prepare phase where participants acknowledge the decision before execution. If coordinator fails, participants can communicate to determine the global state and proceed.

flashcards.front: What is a prepare message in 2PC?
flashcards.back: The prepare message (VOTE-REQUEST) is sent by coordinator asking participants to prepare to commit. Participants must write a prepare log before voting commit.

flashcards.front: What is the difference between 2PC and 3PC?
flashcards.back: 2PC blocks on coordinator failure; 3PC is non-blocking. 3PC has an extra PreCommit phase. 2PC has 2 rounds; 3PC has 3 rounds of messaging.
If coordinator times out waiting for votes, it sends GLOBAL-ABORT. If participant times out waiting for decision, it blocks until coordinator recovers.

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of write-ahead logging in 2PC?
flashcards.back: Participants write undo and redo logs before voting. If failure occurs, logs enable recovery - either commit (redo) or abort (undo) after coordinator decision is known.

flashcards.front: What happens when a participant votes ABORT in 2PC?
flashcards.back: When any participant votes ABORT, coordinator immediately decides GLOBAL-ABORT and sends it to all participants. All participants must abort the transaction.

flashcards.front: What are the three phases in 3PC?
flashcards.back: 1) CanCommit: Coordinator asks participants if they can commit. 2) PreCommit: Coordinator sends pre-commit if all can commit. 3) DoCommit: Participants actually commit the transaction.
